prompt_md: You must forecast the monthly cost of an **AI PR-review bot** and make sure each review fits the model's context window. Input and output are priced separately (output dearer), code tokenizes heavily, and some content is in Turkish. **Pick the best approach and the correct reason.**

decision_choices: Estimate with the 100-tokens-≈-75-words rule on the English text and assume that's the bill

Count representative diffs with the chosen model's own tokenizer, forecast cost from request volume × (input + output) tokens with output weighted higher, add the non-English overhead, and chunk oversized PRs to protect the output reserve
Just set a fixed monthly spend cap and don't bother forecasting tokens
Assume cost scales with the number of PRs only, since diff size doesn't really matter
reason_choices: Because cost is tokens × price with input and output billed separately, code and Turkish tokenize heavier than a rough rule predicts, and the window holds input plus output — so exact counting, an output-weighted forecast, and chunking large diffs give a defensible budget that also fits the context window
Because the 100-tokens-≈-75-words rule is exact enough for any budget and language
Because a fixed spend cap removes any need to understand what drives the cost
Because the number of PRs is the only thing that affects both cost and context usage
